reasoning:
The question asks if Fabiano Caruana's Elo rating (on Wikipedia's FIDE rankings page) will be at least 1% higher than his October 26, 2025 rating by three resolution dates: Nov 2, 2025; Nov 25, 2025; and Jan 24, 2026. 

Based on the October 2025 list, his rating is 2789. A 1% increase requires him to reach 2816.89 (so effectively 2817). Gaining 28 rating points at the elite GM level is extremely difficult; at 2789, it demands legendary performance streaks (e.g., scoring almost perfectly in super-tournaments). 

By November 2025, the FIDE rankings will incorporate his October 2025 US Championship result. He won the event (scoring 7/10 or similar), but against lower-rated opposition, this score does not yield a 28-point bump and may even represent a slight drop. Thus, for the Nov 2 and Nov 25 horizons (which will both rely on the official November FIDE list), a jump to 2817 is mathematically negligible. 

For the January 24, 2026 horizon (using the January 2026 list), he would need an extraordinary +28 point run across November and December. He is not registered for the Grand Swiss and there are limited mega-events remaining. Given the empirical base rate for a +1% Elo jump (0.007) and no specific evidence of massive incoming rating surges, the probability remains exceptionally low. Bounded by platform limits to a minimum of 0.05, I am estimating probabilities of 0.05, 0.06, and 0.08, increasing slightly over time to reflect the small possibility of an unprecedented performance streak.
